findings.evidence: Lines 67-69 vs 82-85 — same signature, `solution_unique := eq_solution_of_forall_apply_eq hB ℓ hu`.
findings.fix: Remove `solution_unique` and keep `eq_solution_of_forall_apply_eq` (or vice versa); do not expose both.
findings.issue: `solution_unique` has the identical statement and hypotheses as `eq_solution_of_forall_apply_eq` (both: `(hB) (ℓ) {u} (hu : ∀ v, B u v = ℓ v) : u = solution hB ℓ`); its body is just `eq_solution_of_forall_apply_eq hB ℓ hu`. This is a redundant public name.

findings.fix: Annotate `solution_spec` `@[simp]` (and/or `@[grind]`); LHS `B (solution hB ℓ) v` is a clean normal form that rewrites to `ℓ v` without looping.
findings.issue: `solution_spec : B (solution hB ℓ) v = ℓ v` is the defining elimination lemma of `solution`, but carries no `@[simp]`/`@[grind]`, so consumers must invoke it by hand to discharge `B (solution …) v = ℓ v` goals.

findings.evidence: Lines 36-37 define it as `(toDual ℝ V).symm ℓ`; used only at lines 51, 56-58, 111.
findings.fix: Make `rieszRepresentative` (and its lemma) `private`, or drop it and inline `(toDual ℝ V).symm`, to keep the surface minimal.
findings.issue: `rieszRepresentative` is a public rename of the existing `(toDual ℝ V).symm` and is only used internally by `solution`/`solutionCLM`; the named downstream target (Lane D weak solution) consumes `solution`/`existsUnique_forall_apply_eq`, not this helper.

summary: Bodies are correctly unexposed and the existence/uniqueness characterization is present, but the surface carries an exact duplicate lemma and the central characteristic lemma lacks an automation annotation.
